events.name: Example: Large Token Unlock
events.description: Illustrative event card showing how the terminal stores pre-event setup, likely reaction, contrarian read, execution plan, and invalidation logic.
events.likelyReaction: Market sells ahead of unlock if float expansion is large, holders are in profit, and liquidity is thin.
events.contrarianReaction: If the unlock is already hedged or OTC demand absorbs supply, the event can become a relief rally.
events.preTrade: Track borrow/funding, spot liquidity, holder distribution, and prior unlock reactions two weeks before the event.
events.tradeDuring: Avoid reflexive execution. Watch spot CVD, perp funding reset, and exchange inflows.
events.exitCondition: Close the event trade once supply impact is absorbed or the market stops reacting to unlock-related flows.
events.riskCase: Headline unlock size overstates actual sell pressure because recipients are long-term holders or already hedged.
